tournament.name: Duress Crew Presents: New England Premodern Winter Regional 2026

decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to utilize the Replenish card as the centerpiece of a powerful combo strategy. By using cards like Attunement and Frantic Search to fill the graveyard with enchantments, players can then cast Replenish to bring them all back onto the battlefield. Other key cards like Opalescence and Parallax Wave help control the board and further enhance the combo potential. The deck also includes disruptive spells like Meddling Mage and Decree of Silence to protect the combo and control the opponent's resources.

The deck is centered around quickly assembling the combo pieces needed to activate Replenish, which allows the player to bring back powerful enchantments such as Opalescence and Parallax Wave from the graveyard. By utilizing cards like Attunement and Intuition to fill the graveyard and combo pieces like Decree of Silence to disrupt the opponent's strategy, the deck aims to control the game while setting up for a game-winning Replenish. This combo-focused strategy is further supported by disruptive elements like Meddling Mage and Tsabo's Web to shut down opposing plays and secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a horde of aggressive Goblin creatures. Using efficient mana acceleration and card draw from Goblin Matron and Goblin Ringleader, the deck aims to control the battlefield with cards like Goblin Piledriver and Siege-Gang Commander. Additionally, the inclusion of Gempalm Incinerator and Goblin Sharpshooter provides removal options for dealing with opposing threats. With a strong mana base and disruptive land choices such as Rishadan Port and Wasteland, the deck can maintain pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on using control elements and survival tactics to outlast opponents. By utilizing cards like Captain Sisay and Survival of the Fittest, the deck can search for key creatures like Deranged Hermit and Silvos, Rogue Elemental to stay alive. With a mix of removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and defensive creatures like Spore Frog and Wall of Roots, the deck aims to control the game until it can overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures like Kamahl, Fist of Krosa and Multani, Maro-Sorcerer.

The main focus of this deck is to assemble an engine where enchantments provide card draw and mana acceleration, enabling the player to cast multiple spells in a turn. The key win condition is to use the Opalescence and Parallax Wave combo to exile all of the opponent's permanents, leaving them with no resources to defend themselves. The deck also includes disruption and protection elements like Swords to Plowshares and Solitary Confinement to ensure the combo can be executed successfully. Additionally, the deck utilizes Serra's Sanctum to generate large amounts of mana to power up its combos.

The deck revolves around utilizing cards like Replenish and Attunement to quickly fill the graveyard with enchantments, then bring them back onto the battlefield to create a powerful board presence. Key cards like Parallax Wave and Opalescence allow for various combo interactions, while cards like Tsabo's Web and Decree of Silence provide disruption for the opponent. The deck aims to control the game early on before setting up a game-winning combo with its enchantment-based synergy.

The deck focuses on using the Phyrexian Dreadnought combo with Stifle to create a powerful win condition. It includes a selection of card draw spells and counterspells to protect and enable the combo, as well as tools like Peek and Portent to dig for key pieces. This deck aims to efficiently control the game while assembling its combo pieces, utilizing versatile cards like Gush and Vision Charm to gain advantage. It seeks to disrupt the opponent's game plan and quickly close out the game with the Dreadnought combo.

The deck focuses on quickly establishing board dominance by aggressively playing creatures like Nimble Mongoose and Terravore while utilizing land destruction spells like Armageddon and Terrageddon to disrupt opponent's strategy. It also includes cards like Weathered Wayfarer and Sylvan Library to help ensure consistent land drops and card advantage. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with efficient threats and disruption, while utilizing the synergy between creatures and land destruction to maintain control of the game.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Duress and Meddling Mage, while also utilizing powerful control spells like Mana Leak and Dromar's Charm. The deck aims to maintain board control through removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Wrath of God, while also generating card advantage with cards like Fact or Fiction and Impulse. The deck also has access to powerful finishers like Exalted Angel and Crypt Angel to close out the game. Overall, the deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver the opponent through a mix of disruption and powerful threats.

This deck focuses on utilizing the mana generation abilities of elves with cards like Priest of Titania and Gaea's Cradle to quickly ramp up and play big threats like Deranged Hermit and Masticore. The deck also includes cards like Survival of the Fittest to tutor for key creatures and maintain board presence. The inclusion of cards like Quirion Ranger and Wirewood Symbiote allow for flexible mana usage and protection for key creatures. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive elf tribal strategy.

The main focus of this deck is centered around cards like Psychatog and Meddling Mage to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategies. Utilizing a combination of counterspells and removal spells like Swords to Plowshares, the deck aims to maintain control over the board while using card draw spells like Gush and Accumulated Knowledge to maintain card advantage. Additionally, the deck includes some disruptive elements like Duress and Armageddon to further disrupt the opponent's game plan. The mana base is designed to support the deck's color requirements and maintain consistency.

The deck focuses on using various control elements such as counter spells and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan while building up card advantage through draw spells like Accumulated Knowledge and Gush. Psychatog is the primary win condition, utilizing its ability to grow in power based on cards in the graveyard. Winter Orb is used to slow down the opponent's mana development, giving the deck time to establish control. The deck aims to outlast the opponent and then finish them off with a sizable Psychatog or by grinding them out with attrition.

The premise of this deck is to quickly get out Phyrexian Dreadnought and use Stifle to prevent the sacrifice effect, creating a powerful 12/12 creature for one mana. The deck also includes counterspells and card draw to stall and find key pieces, as well as control elements like Powder Keg to handle threats. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces efficiently and protect it until the opponent is overwhelmed by the massive creature. By effectively managing resources and disrupting opponents, this deck aims to close out games swiftly and decisively.

This deck revolves around using cards like Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful combo that can quickly take out opponents. By using counterspells and disruption spells, the deck aims to control the game and prevent the opponent from building a strong board presence. The deck also includes draw spells like Accumulated Knowledge and Portent to maintain card advantage. Overall, the goal is to assemble the combo pieces efficiently while disrupting the opponent's game plan to secure a win.

The deck focuses on using creature-based control elements, such as Wall of Blossoms and Nantuko Vigilante, along with graveyard manipulation through Survival of the Fittest. The deck aims to slowly build up its board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thornscape Battlemage and Goblin Bombardment. The inclusion of utility creatures like Skyshroud Poacher and Caller of the Claw allows for flexibility in different situations. The deck utilizes Gaea's Cradle and mana dorks like Llanowar Elves to ramp up quickly and overwhelm the opponent with strong creatures like Deranged Hermit and Multani's Acolyte.

The deck focuses on using welding abilities to manipulate artifacts and control the board using cards like Goblin Welder and Tangle Wire. It also utilizes the Tinker card to quickly bring out powerful artifacts like Phyrexian Devourer and Triskelion. The deck aims to disrupt opponent's strategies by locking down the board with Winter Orb and Sphere of Resistance, while generating mana advantage with cards like Thran Dynamo and Ancient Tomb. Overall, the deck's strategy revolves around controlling the game state and outvaluing opponents with efficient artifact plays.

The focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm opponents with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells in a Mono Black Aggro strategy. By combining cheap creatures like Rotlung Reanimator and Priest of Gix with powerful disruptive spells like Duress and Cabal Therapy, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure on the board. The inclusion of cards like Graveborn Muse and Cabal Archon provides additional value and ways to close out the game. With a strong mana base including City of Traitors and Rishadan Port, this deck aims for a fast and relentless assault on the opponent.

This deck focuses on putting pressure on the opponent early with aggressive creatures like Ravenous Rats and Call of the Herd, while also disrupting their game plan with hand disruption spells like Duress and Cabal Therapy. The deck also includes removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Terminate to deal with opposing threats. The inclusion of cards like Sylvan Library and Treetop Village provide additional card advantage and utility. The main goal is to chip away at the opponent's life total while controlling the board to secure a victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing enchantress cards to draw more cards and generate mana, while using combo pieces such as Opalescence and Parallax Wave to control the board and eventually win the game. It aims to create a loop of enchantment triggers and generate a powerful board presence. The deck includes protection and card advantage through cards like Sterling Grove and Solitary Confinement. By combining enchantress abilities with powerful enchantments like Serra's Sanctum, the deck can quickly overwhelm opponents and secure victory through its combo interactions.

The deck revolves around aggressively deploying goblins onto the battlefield to overwhelm the opponent with speed and numbers. Utilizing creatures with abilities such as haste, pump effects, and tribal synergies, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total before they have a chance to stabilize. Key cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron allow for efficient searching and cheating creatures into play, while cards like Goblin Ringleader provide card advantage to keep the pressure on. The deck also includes disruption in the form of land destruction and artifact removal to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with low-cost aggressive creatures like Savannah Lions and Wild Mongrel, supported by Madness cards like Basking Rootwalla and Arrogant Wurm. The strategy revolves around using cards like Swords to Plowshares and Cursed Scroll for removal while utilizing cards like Glory and Tireless Tribe to maintain board presence and pressure. The mana base is designed to support a fast-paced aggro strategy with a mix of basic lands and utility lands like Mishra's Factory and Wasteland.

The main objective of this deck is to flood the board with aggressive creatures like Anurid Brushhopper and Exalted Angel, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Meddling Mage and Swords to Plowshares. Mother of Runes provides protection for key creatures, while Weathered Wayfarer accelerates mana. The deck also includes some disruptive spells like Mox Diamond and Armageddon, as well as card advantage engines like Deep Analysis and Quiet Speculation. The mana base includes powerful lands like City of Brass and Wasteland to support the deck's aggressive strategy.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using various creatures and spells to survive against opponents. The deck includes a combination of creatures with powerful abilities, such as Nantuko Vigilante and Ravenous Baloth, as well as spells like Duress and Cabal Therapy to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also focuses on utilizing the Survival of the Fittest card to search for and retrieve key creatures from the deck. By maintaining control of the game and outlasting the opponent, this deck aims to secure victory through strategic gameplay.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and aggressively put pressure on the opponent using efficient creatures like River Boa, Mogg Fanatic, and Wild Mongrel, while also using burn spells like Incinerate and Lightning Bolt to finish off the opponent. The deck also utilizes disruption with cards like Naturalize and Pyrostatic Pillar to control the board and protect its creatures. The mana base consists of a mix of lands that provide both red and green mana sources, along with some utility lands like Treetop Village and Wasteland to further support the aggressive gameplay.

The core idea of this deck is to aggressively deal damage to the opponent as quickly as possible through efficient and low-cost creatures and burn spells. The focus is on applying pressure early in the game and maintaining that pressure to finish off the opponent before they can stabilize. Control of the board is achieved through burn spells and creature removal, while cards like Black Vise and Pyrostatic Pillar punish the opponent for playing spells. The deck aims to win by overwhelming the opponent with a fast and unrelenting stream of damage.

This deck revolves around creating a lock on the opponent using the Stasis card, which prevents them from untapping their lands. The key strategy involves keeping the opponent's resources limited through cards like Black Vise and Powder Keg while countering any threats with cards like Counterspell and Daze. The deck aims to control the game and stall until it can find a win condition, typically through a combo involving Gush and other card draw spells. It utilizes Forsaken City to keep Stasis active and maintain the lockdown on the opponent.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the "Stiflenought" combo, which involves playing Phyrexian Dreadnought and then using Stifle to counteract the negative effects of its ability. The deck also includes a variety of control and card draw spells to help manipulate the game in your favor. By combining efficient creature removal with powerful card draw and disruption spells, the deck aims to outmaneuver and outpace opponent decks in a Pre-modern 1 vs 1 format.

This deck focuses on assembling the powerful combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a fast win condition. By utilizing cards like Counterspell, Foil, and Thwart to protect the combo, as well as card draw spells like Accumulated Knowledge and Gush to find the pieces quickly, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win. Powder Keg provides additional control options, while Vision Charm and Peek offer additional card selection. Overall, the deck aims to quickly deploy its combo while controlling the game.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle combo to create a powerful threat on the board. By combining Stifle's ability to counter Dreadnought's drawback with countermagic and card draw, this deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining card advantage. With additional disruption tools like Powder Keg and Thwart, as well as card selection from Peek and Impulse, this deck aims to control the game until it can assemble its combo and overwhelm the opponent.

The deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures like Nimble Mongoose and Terravore to quickly deal damage to the opponent while maintaining board control with cards like Swords to Plowshares and Armageddon. The inclusion of cards like Mox Diamond and Weathered Wayfarer allows for fast mana acceleration and land destruction, while Sylvan Library helps with card advantage. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its quick and efficient threats, while also disrupting their mana base with Wasteland and Rishadan Port.

The deck focuses on using defensive creatures and disruptive spells to control the game. It aims to gradually gain advantage through card advantage and removal, eventually overwhelming the opponent with powerful threats like Blastoderm, Deranged Hermit, and Ravenous Baloth. The deck also utilizes the synergy between creatures like Wall of Roots and Pernicious Deed to control the board. Recurring Nightmare provides additional value by allowing the player to repeatedly bring back key creatures from the graveyard. The overall strategy is to outlast the opponent and win through attrition.

This deck revolves around utilizing Tinker to search for powerful artifacts like Phyrexian Devourer and Altar of Dementia, which can be used to create a powerful combo with Fling to deal a massive amount of damage to the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Defense Grid and Tsabo's Web to disrupt the opponent's game plan and buy time to assemble the combo. The mana base is designed to provide the necessary resources to execute the combo efficiently.
